synonyms.sql: COUNT(DISTINCT user_pseudo_id) or COUNT(DISTINCT merged_user_id) for cross-device
SUM(ecommerce.purchase_revenue) WHERE event_name = 'purchase' for transaction-level. For product-level revenue: SUM(items.item_revenue) after CROSS JOIN UNNEST(items) WHERE event_name = 'purchase'

CROSS JOIN UNNEST(items) to access product-level data. Use items.item_name, items.item_category for identification, items.item_revenue for revenue, items.quantity for units sold. Filter to event_name = 'purchase' for sold products
page_location (full URL) or page.hostname, page.path, page.query (parsed components)

Drill-down hierarchy: (1) .primary_channel_group is the default — always use when asked vaguely about traffic sources or channels. (2) .source and .medium — use when asked for source/medium breakdown. (3) .campaign_name — use when asked for campaign-level detail. All under session_traffic_source_last_click.cross_channel_campaign.

keyFields.note: Partition column. Always include in WHERE to limit scanned data and cost.
Device-level user identifier. COUNT(DISTINCT) for unique users.
Unique session identifier. COUNT(DISTINCT) for sessions, GROUP BY for session metrics.
Cross-device user identifier. Prefer over user_pseudo_id for user-level analysis.
Full page URL. Use page.hostname and page.path for structured analysis.
Transaction-level revenue. Filter to event_name = 'purchase' before aggregating.
Product-level data (nested array). CROSS JOIN UNNEST(items) to access item_name, item_category, item_revenue, quantity. Filter to event_name = 'purchase' for sold products.

Primary field for traffic source analysis. When a user asks a general or vague question about 'traffic sources' or 'where traffic comes from', DEFAULT to .cross_channel_campaign.primary_channel_group. Only drill down to .source if the user explicitly mentions 'source' or specific site names.
Event timestamp in the configured timezone. Use for time-of-day analysis.
Data stability flag. WHERE data_is_final = true for stable data only.

filteringGuidance.text: Always filter on event_date (partition column) to reduce query cost.
Use event_name to select specific event types (e.g. WHERE event_name = 'page_view').
For user counts: COUNT(DISTINCT user_pseudo_id) for device-level, COUNT(DISTINCT merged_user_id) for cross-device.
For session metrics: GROUP BY session_id.
For transaction-level ecommerce revenue: filter WHERE event_name = 'purchase' before aggregating ecommerce.purchase_revenue.

For product-level analysis: CROSS JOIN UNNEST(items) WHERE event_name = 'purchase'. Use items.item_revenue for product revenue, items.quantity for units sold, items.item_category for product categories.
For traffic source analysis: use session_traffic_source_last_click.cross_channel_campaign.primary_channel_group for high-level traffic source information
Nested arrays (event_params, items): use CROSS JOIN UNNEST(...) to access individual values.
Use data_is_final = true to exclude data that may still change in future refreshes.
